name: Brownie Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate over hot water.
2. Sift the flour, baking soda, and baking powder together. Set aside.
3. In a bowl of a mixer, cream the butter. Add the sugar slowly and continue to beat. Add the vanilla extract, then the eggs one at a time. Add the melted chocolate and stir to blend.
4. Stir in the dry ingredients by hand. Mix in the milk. When just blended, mix in the chopped walnuts. Cover and refrigerate dough for 30 minutes before rolling out.
5. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Lightly grease a cookie sheet.
6. To form the cookies, scoop out a portion of dough and roll it out on a well-floured surface until it is about 1/4 inch thick. Cut into rounds or other desired shapes. Re-roll scraps and cut again.
7. Place the cookies on the prepared cookie sheet (leaving room for expansion) and bake for 6 to 8 minutes, watching for hot spots in the oven so they do not overbrown. Variation: For a brownie cookie crust, chill the dough briefly, lightly grease a large baking sheet, roll out the dough as thinly as possible, bake until crumbly, crush into fine crumbs (about 2 1/4 cups), melt 1 stick plus 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter, mix with the crumbs and pack over the bottom and up the sides of a 10-inch springform pan. Freeze for 30 minutes before filling. For individual crusts, line a muffin pan with waxed paper and follow the same steps.
